meta.name: Microsoft 365 Fundamentals (MS-900)
meta.description: Foundational Microsoft 365 cloud service concepts for the MS-900 certification exam.

cards.definition: Office 365 is the original subscription suite of productivity apps (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams) plus cloud services. Microsoft 365 is a superset that adds Windows 11 Enterprise licenses, advanced security (Microsoft Defender, Intune MDM/MAM), and compliance tools. Microsoft 365 Business and Enterprise plans include everything in Office 365 plus device and identity management.
cards.hint: Microsoft 365 = Office 365 + Windows + enterprise security and management.

cards.definition: The hub for teamwork in Microsoft 365. Integrates chat, video meetings, file sharing (via SharePoint), and app integrations into one workspace. Teams are backed by Microsoft 365 Groups, which provision a SharePoint site, Exchange mailbox, and OneNote. Channels organise conversations by topic. Used for remote collaboration, meetings, webinars, and phone calls (Teams Phone add-on).
cards.hint: Chat + meetings + files + apps - all in one place for your team.

cards.definition: Microsoft's cloud-based content management and collaboration platform. Used to create intranet sites, store and share documents, and build team sites and communication sites. Deeply integrated with Teams (each team gets a SharePoint site), OneDrive (personal libraries), and Power Automate. Offers document libraries, lists, and rich web-part-based pages.
cards.hint: The intranet and document management backbone of Microsoft 365.

cards.term: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) in Microsoft 365
cards.definition: Every Microsoft 365 tenant is backed by a Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) tenant for identity and access management. It authenticates all users, enforces Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), Conditional Access policies, and Single Sign-On (SSO) across all Microsoft 365 services and connected third-party SaaS apps. User, group, and licence management all flow through Entra ID.
cards.hint: The identity backbone of every Microsoft 365 organisation.

cards.definition: A cloud-based Mobile Device Management (MDM) and Mobile Application Management (MAM) service included in Microsoft 365. Allows IT to enrol and manage Windows, iOS, Android, and macOS devices, enforce compliance policies (PIN, encryption), push apps, and wipe devices remotely. Part of Microsoft Endpoint Manager along with Configuration Manager.
cards.hint: The IT control panel for managing every device and app in your organisation from the cloud.

cards.definition: A unified portal for managing data governance, risk, and compliance across Microsoft 365. Key features: Sensitivity Labels (classify and protect documents/emails), Retention Policies (keep or delete content per regulatory requirements), eDiscovery (legal hold and search), Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies, and Communication Compliance. Helps meet GDPR, HIPAA, and other regulatory requirements.
cards.hint: The admin hub for classifying, protecting, and governing your organisation's data.

cards.definition: Business plans (up to 300 users): Business Basic (web apps + Teams), Business Standard (desktop apps + Teams), Business Premium (adds Intune, Defender). Enterprise plans (unlimited users): F1/F3 (Firstline Workers), E3 (full desktop + compliance), E5 (adds advanced security, analytics, voice). Add-ons include Microsoft Copilot, Teams Phone, and Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.
cards.hint: Business = SMB (≤300 users). Enterprise = large organisations. E5 = the premium everything bundle.

cards.definition: A suite of cloud-based security services protecting Microsoft 365 workloads. Microsoft Defender for Office 365 protects email and collaboration (anti-phishing, safe links, safe attachments). Microsoft Defender for Endpoint secures Windows/macOS/Linux devices. Microsoft Defender for Identity protects on-premises Active Directory. Microsoft Defender XDR correlates signals across all these for unified incident investigation.
cards.hint: The security layer guarding your Microsoft 365 email, devices, and identities from attacks.

cards.definition: Each Microsoft 365 user gets 1 TB (or more) of cloud storage through OneDrive for Business, backed by SharePoint infrastructure. Files sync to Windows/macOS for offline access. Users can share files internally or externally with configurable permissions. Known Folder Move (KFM) automatically backs up Desktop, Documents, and Pictures. Different from personal OneDrive (consumer).
cards.hint: Your personal cloud drive in Microsoft 365 - synced, backed up, and shareable.

cards.definition: Microsoft guarantees 99.9% uptime SLA for Microsoft 365 (financially backed). Service health is monitored in the Microsoft 365 Admin Centre → Service Health dashboard, showing active incidents, advisories, and history. The Microsoft Service Trust Portal provides compliance documentation, audit reports, and data protection information for regulatory due diligence.
cards.hint: 99.9% uptime promise, with a live health dashboard so you always know the service status.

cards.definition: The suite of productivity applications included with Microsoft 365: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote, and Access (PC only). Available as web apps, mobile apps, and desktop apps depending on the licence. Desktop apps require Business Standard, Business Premium, E3, or E5.
cards.hint: Word, Excel, PowerPoint - in web, mobile, or desktop form depending on your plan.

cards.definition: A membership service that connects users across Microsoft 365. Creating a Group automatically provisions a shared mailbox, SharePoint site, Planner board, OneNote, and Teams workspace (if enabled). Groups simplify collaboration and permissions.
cards.hint: The membership engine behind Teams, SharePoint, Planner, and more.

cards.definition: Microsoft's cloud-hosted email and calendaring service. Provides 50–100 GB mailboxes, shared mailboxes, anti-malware filtering, retention policies, and Outlook integration. Supports hybrid deployments with on-prem Exchange.
cards.hint: Enterprise-grade email and calendars in the cloud.

cards.definition: A lightweight task and project management tool integrated with Microsoft 365 Groups. Provides Kanban-style boards, task assignments, due dates, and progress tracking. Often used for team task management.
cards.hint: Simple Kanban boards for team tasks.

cards.definition: A collaborative workspace for real-time co-authoring. Loop components (tables, lists, tasks) stay in sync across Teams, Outlook, and other apps. Loop workspaces help teams organize ideas and content.
cards.hint: Real-time collaborative components that sync everywhere.

cards.definition: An employee experience platform integrated into Microsoft Teams. Modules include Viva Connections (intranet), Viva Learning (training), Viva Insights (productivity analytics), Viva Goals (OKRs), and Viva Engage (community).
cards.hint: Employee experience tools built directly into Teams.

cards.definition: A Microsoft security framework that assumes no implicit trust. Every access request must be authenticated, authorized, and continuously validated. Core principles: verify explicitly, use least privilege, assume breach.
cards.hint: Never trust - always verify.

cards.definition: A Microsoft Entra ID feature that enforces access rules based on user, device, location, risk level, and app. Common policies: require MFA, block legacy authentication, enforce compliant devices.
cards.hint: If X condition is true, then allow or block access.

cards.definition: Adds a second layer of authentication beyond passwords. Methods include Microsoft Authenticator app, SMS codes, phone calls, and FIDO2 keys. Required for Zero Trust and recommended for all users.
cards.hint: Something you know + something you have.

cards.definition: Policies that detect and prevent sharing of sensitive information (credit cards, SSNs, health data). Applies to Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams. Can block, warn, or audit risky actions.
cards.hint: Stops sensitive data from leaking outside the organisation.

cards.definition: Labels that classify and protect documents and emails. Can apply encryption, watermarking, access restrictions, and automatic classification. Managed in the Microsoft Purview Compliance Portal.
cards.hint: Confidential, Highly Confidential - with automatic protection.

cards.definition: Policies that determine how long content is kept or deleted across Microsoft 365. Used for regulatory compliance, legal requirements, and information governance.
cards.hint: Keep content for as long as required - delete when appropriate.

cards.definition: Tools for legal teams to search, preserve, and export content across Microsoft 365. Supports legal hold, case management, and advanced search queries.
cards.hint: Search and preserve data for legal investigations.

cards.definition: A security analytics tool that evaluates your organisation's security posture and recommends improvements. Higher scores indicate stronger security configurations.
cards.hint: A security grade for your Microsoft 365 environment.

cards.definition: The central portal for managing users, groups, licenses, billing, and service health. Specialized admin centers exist for Teams, SharePoint, Exchange, and Security.
cards.hint: The main dashboard for managing Microsoft 365.

cards.definition: Microsoft 365 is a SaaS offering. Azure provides IaaS (VMs, storage), PaaS (App Service, SQL Database), and SaaS (Microsoft 365 apps). MS-900 requires understanding the differences between these cloud models.
cards.hint: IaaS = infrastructure. PaaS = platform. SaaS = ready-to-use apps.

cards.definition: Microsoft manages the infrastructure, uptime, and security of the Microsoft 365 cloud. Customers manage data, identities, devices, and access policies. Security responsibilities shift depending on the service.
cards.hint: Microsoft secures the cloud; you secure your data and users.

cards.definition: An AI assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 apps. Helps draft emails, summarize meetings, analyze Excel data, generate PowerPoint slides, and answer questions using your organizational data. Requires Microsoft 365 E3/E5 or Business Standard/Premium plus a Copilot license.
cards.hint: AI built into Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams.

cards.definition: An add-on that turns Microsoft Teams into a full cloud-based phone system. Supports PSTN calling, auto attendants, call queues, voicemail, and number porting. Requires Teams Phone license and calling plan or Direct Routing.
cards.hint: Use Teams as your organisation's phone system.

cards.definition: Microsoft provides platform-level redundancy and retention, but customers are responsible for long-term backup needs. Third-party backup solutions are often used for extended retention, point-in-time restore, and compliance.
cards.hint: Microsoft protects the service - you protect your long-term data.

cards.definition: A dedicated instance of Microsoft 365 services assigned to an organization. Each tenant has its own Entra ID directory, domain configuration, licensing pool, and data boundary. Tenants are isolated from one another for security and compliance.
cards.hint: Your organization's private Microsoft 365 environment.

cards.definition: Organizations can add custom domains (e.g., company.com) to personalize email addresses and Teams identities. DNS records (MX, TXT, CNAME) must be configured for verification and mail routing.
cards.hint: Use your own company domain for email and Teams.

cards.definition: Built-in email filtering that protects against spam, malware, and phishing. Included in all Microsoft 365 plans. Provides anti-spoofing, quarantine, and connection filtering.
cards.hint: Email security that blocks spam and malicious messages.

cards.definition: Advanced email and collaboration protection. Features include Safe Links, Safe Attachments, anti-phishing policies, and attack simulation training. Included in Microsoft 365 E5 or as an add-on.
cards.hint: Advanced protection for email, OneDrive, SharePoint, and Teams.

cards.definition: Endpoint detection and response (EDR) solution that protects Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android devices. Provides threat detection, vulnerability management, and automated remediation.
cards.hint: Advanced security for laptops, desktops, and mobile devices.

cards.definition: Secure Score provides prioritized recommendations such as enabling MFA, blocking legacy authentication, configuring DLP, and applying sensitivity labels. Each action increases the organization's security posture.
cards.hint: A checklist of actions to strengthen your security.

cards.definition: Encrypts emails so only intended recipients can read them. Works with Outlook, Gmail, and other providers. Users can encrypt messages with sensitivity labels or manually via Outlook.
cards.hint: Encrypt emails to protect sensitive information.

cards.definition: Microsoft stores customer data in regional datacenters based on the tenant's geography. Data residency ensures compliance with local regulations such as GDPR.
cards.hint: Your Microsoft 365 data stays in your region.

cards.definition: An add-on that allows organizations to store data in multiple geographic locations within a single tenant. Useful for multinational companies with data residency requirements.
cards.hint: Store data in multiple regions within one tenant.

cards.definition: Microsoft recommends direct internet connections for Microsoft 365 traffic, avoiding VPN hairpinning. Uses local DNS resolution and optimized routing to Microsoft's global network.
cards.hint: Direct, local internet breakout improves Microsoft 365 performance.

cards.definition: Update channels determine how frequently Office apps receive new features: Current Channel (monthly), Monthly Enterprise Channel, and Semi-Annual Enterprise Channel (twice per year).
cards.hint: Choose how often Office apps get updates.

cards.definition: Microsoft provides a financially backed 99.9% uptime SLA for Microsoft 365 services. If uptime falls below SLA, customers may receive service credits.
cards.hint: Microsoft guarantees 99.9% availability for Microsoft 365.

cards.definition: Retention policies preserve or delete data based on rules. Backup solutions create point-in-time copies for recovery. Microsoft 365 includes retention but not full backup; third-party backup is often required.
cards.hint: Retention ≠ backup - both serve different purposes.

cards.definition: The enterprise version of Office apps with advanced features like shared computer activation, enterprise licensing, and integration with Microsoft 365 E3/E5.
cards.hint: The enterprise-grade version of Office desktop apps.

cards.definition: A video platform integrated with SharePoint and OneDrive. Supports meeting recordings, screen captures, transcripts, and video sharing with organizational permissions.
cards.hint: Store and share videos securely inside Microsoft 365.

cards.definition: A collaborative digital canvas for brainstorming, diagrams, and workshops. Integrated with Teams meetings for real-time co-authoring.
cards.hint: A shared digital whiteboard for teams.

cards.definition: An online scheduling tool that integrates with Outlook calendars. Customers can book appointments through a web page. Useful for service-based businesses.
cards.hint: Let customers book appointments directly online.

cards.definition: A lightweight survey and quiz tool for collecting feedback, polls, and assessments. Responses export to Excel and integrate with Power Automate.
cards.hint: Create surveys and quizzes in minutes.

cards.definition: A web-based storytelling and presentation tool for creating interactive reports, newsletters, and presentations. Content is automatically formatted for web and mobile.
cards.hint: Create interactive, web-based presentations easily.

cards.definition: A customizable training portal built on SharePoint that provides Microsoft 365 tutorials, onboarding content, and user adoption resources.
cards.hint: A built-in training portal to help users learn Microsoft 365.
